name: Rahmapfelkuchen (Apple and Rum Custard Cake)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak raisins in 1/4 cup rum for half an hour before using.
2. To make the crust, mix flour, sugar, and lemon rind. Cut in butter or margarine until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Add egg yolk and 1 tablespoon milk; mix gently to form a dough. Pat into the bottom of a 10-inch Springform pan with only greased sides. Press dough up the sides of the pan for about 1 inch.
3. Toss together bread crumbs and melted butter. Spread evenly over the pastry crust.
4. Toss apple slices, lemon juice, and 1/4 cup sugar. Spread apples over the crumbs.
5. Drain raisins, reserving rum, and sprinkle raisins over the apples.
6. Beat eggs and sugar until thick and lemon-colored. Stir in milk and reserved rum. Pour custard over apples.
7. Bake in a preheated oven at 350 degrees F for 15 minutes. Then beat eggs and sugar until thick and lemon-colored. Stir in milk and reserved rum. Pour custard over apples and bake for 45 to 60 minutes at 350 degrees F until the custard is set.
8. Cool completely before serving. Do not remove the springform pan until cool.
